title: Section IV.

source.title: ANF05. Fathers of the Third Century: Hippolytus, Cyprian, Caius, Novatian, Appendix

verses.book: A discourse by the most blessed Hippolytus, bishop and martyr, on the end of the world, and on Antichrist, and on the second coming of our lord Jesus Christ.
verses.text: Wherefore let us direct our discourse to a second witness.

verses.book: A discourse by the most blessed Hippolytus, bishop and martyr, on the end of the world, and on Antichrist, and on the second coming of our lord Jesus Christ.
verses.text: And of what sort is this one?

verses.book: A discourse by the most blessed Hippolytus, bishop and martyr, on the end of the world, and on Antichrist, and on the second coming of our lord Jesus Christ.
verses.text: Listen to Osea, as he speaks thus grandly: “In those days the Lord shall bring on a burning wind from the desert against them, and shall make their veins dry, and shall make their springs desolate; and all their goodly vessels shall be spoiled.

verses.book: A discourse by the most blessed Hippolytus, bishop and martyr, on the end of the world, and on Antichrist, and on the second coming of our lord Jesus Christ.
verses.text: Because they rose up against God, they shall fall by the sword, and their women with child shall be ripped up.”

verses.book: A discourse by the most blessed Hippolytus, bishop and martyr, on the end of the world, and on Antichrist, and on the second coming of our lord Jesus Christ.
verses.text: And what else is this burning wind from the east, than the Antichrist that is to destroy and dry up the veins of the waters and the fruits of the trees in his times, because men set their hearts on his works?

verses.book: A discourse by the most blessed Hippolytus, bishop and martyr, on the end of the world, and on Antichrist, and on the second coming of our lord Jesus Christ.
verses.text: For which reason he shall indeed destroy them, and they shall serve him in his pollution.
